records.ety: [F. enchsser; pref. en- (L. in) + chsse box containing relics, frame, case, the same word as caisse case. See 1st Case, and cf. Chase, Encase, Incase.]
records.morph: [imp. & p. p. Enchased (?); p. pr. & vb. n. Enchasing.]

defs: To incase or inclose in a border or rim; to surround with an ornamental casing, as a gem with gold; to encircle; to inclose; to adorn.
quotes: Enchased with a wanton ivy twine.
An precious stones, in studs of gold enchased, The shaggy velvet of his buskins graced.

defs: To chase; to ornament by embossing or engraving; as, to enchase a watch case.
To delineate or describe, as by writing.
quotes: All which . . . for to enchase, Him needeth sure a golden pen, I ween.
